body.title: [triage] PRE-EXISTING RED: test_budget_gate dispatch tests fail at HEAD — the balance check before the OR call is not firing
body.intention: I intend that a red test is never carried as background noise — because a suite with a known-red member cannot tell a NEW regression from the old one, so the next real break arrives invisible.
body.description:
Found 2026-07-13 while proving the sprint-entry intention gate. VERIFIED PRE-EXISTING: reproduced in a clean git worktree at HEAD, so it is NOT a regression from that work.

FAILING:
  tests/test_budget_gate.py::TestDispatchBudgetGate::test_dispatch_checks_balance_before_or_call
  tests/test_budget_gate.py::TestDispatchBudgetGate::test_dispatch_records_spend_after_or_call

WHY IT MATTERS (and why it is not merely untidy): per CLAUDE.md the suite is meant to be green so that A RED IS ALWAYS A REAL REGRESSION. A carried red destroys exactly that property — the signal 'the suite went red' stops distinguishing 'I broke something' from 'that one was already broken', which is the same non-injective-signal defect as R-feedback-is-unconditional. It cost real time today: I had to spin a worktree at HEAD to find out whether I had caused it.

**Affected files:** tests/test_budget_gate.py, unseen_university/devices/inference/ (dispatch balance check).

**Scope boundary:** IN — diagnose whether the test or the code is wrong, and fix the one that is. OUT — redesigning the budget gate.

**Test plan:** the two named nodes go green, and the reason is understood (not silenced with a skip).

**Proof obligation:** the hollow build marks them xfail/skip and reports a green suite — which is the defect, not the fix. What a hollow build could not pass: the two nodes PASS, and a mutation to the balance check turns them red again (proving they test the balance check and not the mock).

**Completion criteria:** 1. Both nodes pass. 2. No skip/xfail added. 3. A deliberate mutation to the balance-check code reddens them.
